questions.question: You fly for 87 minutes at a speed of 159 mph and find yourself 9 nm off course. If you have another 133 nm to go, what is your correction to parallel?
In the above question, what is the total correction to your destination?
questions.context: Referenced question 1: You fly for 87 minutes at a speed of 159 mph and find yourself 9 nm off course. If you have another 133 nm to go, what is your correction to parallel?

questions.question: Given: [[NEXT_LINE]] Winds aloft: 203° @ 23 knots [[NEXT_LINE]] WCA: 10° R [[NEXT_LINE]] TAS: 121 knots [[NEXT_LINE]] MH: 135° [[NEXT_LINE]] Variation: 7° W [[NEXT_LINE]] Distance flown: 211 sm [[NEXT_LINE]] Fuel flow: 8.4 gph [[NEXT_LINE]] Deviation: -4° [[NEXT_LINE]] How much gas was used for this flight?
An airplane descends from 9500 feet to 2800 feet at a rate of 830 fpm. It traveled 34.7 sm during the descent. What was its ground speed?
You fly 1 hour and 53 minutes covering 319 sm with a TAS of 151 knots If your TC is 256° and your TH is 247°, what is the wind speed aloft?

You decide to live on the edge and fly a Cessna 172 from Palm Beach International, Florida to Grand Bahama International, Bahamas. Using the following information, how far from Grand Bahama will you be when it will take just as long to continue to Grand Bahama as it will to turn around and go back to Palm Beach? [[NEXT_LINE]] Distance between airports: 76 nm [[NEXT_LINE]] Pressure Altitude: 7,000' MSL [[NEXT_LINE]] Indicated Airspeed: 116 KIAS [[NEXT_LINE]] OAT: 6°C [[NEXT_LINE]] True Course from Palm Beach: 095° [[NEXT_LINE]] True Wind: 302° at 39 mph [[NEXT_LINE]] Fuel On Board/Fuel Flow: 48 US gallons / 63 lb./hr.
Surface winds from the last METAR were reported to be 350@23 knots. You plan to take off runway 5. If the variation at the field is 16° east, what is your crosswind component?

You have loaded your aircraft to weigh 3319 lbs. The problem is that your CG of 68.7 inches is 1.1 inches forward of the limit. How many pounds need to be moved from the current CG to station 110.3 to bring you back within limits?
Winds aloft forecast: [[TABLE]] TAS 137 mph; TC 165°; Deviation +3°; Cruise alt. 3500'; MH 179°. What would the variation be?

Given: [[NEXT_LINE]] Winds 168° @ 36 knots [[NEXT_LINE]] Altimeter setting 30.18" [[NEXT_LINE]] Cruise alt. 6500' [[NEXT_LINE]] TC 246° [[NEXT_LINE]] IAS 162 mph [[NEXT_LINE]] OAT 61°F [[NEXT_LINE]] What is your TAS?

You climb at 97 mph. Your Airport Facility Directory indicates that there is a 91 meter obstacle off the end of the runway and you've calculated it to be 1.1 nm from your point of rotation. What climb rate will be required to clear this obstacle by 50'?

Given: [[NEXT_LINE]] Winds = 245° @ 22 Knots [[NEXT_LINE]] Useable fuel = 74 gals. [[NEXT_LINE]] TAS = 119 mph [[NEXT_LINE]] Fuel flow = 74 lbs./hr [[NEXT_LINE]] TC out = 079° [[NEXT_LINE]] How far out can you go under the above circumstances and still make it back with VFR day reserves?
How much does 36 quarts of oil weigh?
How much does 54.4 gallons of fuel weigh?

Your true course is 097°. The winds aloft at your altitude are 360/26 knots and you have a true airspeed of 102 knots. If you have a ground speed of 109 mph, how long will it take you to travel 176 kilometers?
What is the density altitude if the field elevation is 3,543' and the OAT is 72°F? The altimeter setting is 29.92" Hg.
If you burned 92.1 Imperial gallons of fuel on a 6 hour and 32 min flight, what was your burn rate in U.S. gallons?
Your TAS is 348 knots at 23,000' in a standard atmosphere. What is the Mach number?
If you cover 90 nm in 70 minutes, what rate of climb do you need to maintain 427 feet per nautical mile climb gradient?
Your altitude is 17,500' with a temperature of -15 C. What TAS will give you 199 mph IAS?
TC = 096, GS = 137 knots, TH = 101, TAS = 121 knots. Find: Wind correction angle, wind direction, and wind velocity.
TC = 193°, GS = 265 kph, Wind = 080° at 34 mph. Find true heading and TAS.
When Charles Lindberg crossed the Atlantic Ocean in 1927, he flew for 33.5 hours and traveled 3610 statute miles. What was his average speed?

At one point while skimming just 50 feet over the icy seas, his TAS was 110 miles per hour. His GPS indicated a ground speed of 85 knots, and a track of 092°. His state of the art induction compass indicated a heading of 086°. What were the winds?
He carried 451 gallons of fuel, and landed with 85 gallons. What was his average fuel burn?

When he landed in France, 100,000 eager spectators flocked to his airplane. Each was intent on taking a souvenir swatch of the fabric covering. If there were 964 square feet of fabric on the airplane, and each spectator took one piece of fabric that was two square inches in size, how many spectators went home empty handed?

choices.text: None, there were plenty of swatches left

questions.question: Upon retirement, the Spirit of St. Louis had 489 hours 28 minutes of flight time spanning 174 flights. If the average ground speed for the life of the airplane was 85 miles per hour, what was the average distance per flight?

A few years later in 1938, a fellow named Howard decided to show off his fast airplane and flew around the world. His first leg was from Floyd Bennett airport in New York, NY to the same airport in Paris where Lindberg landed. This leg covered 3641 statute miles in a mere 16 hours and 38 minutes. What was his average ground speed?

Fast-forward to 1977 when the crew of an Air France Concorde flew the inaugural trip between New York and Paris. Those smart alecks were really putting old Lindy to shame by cruising at Mach 2.02. If they flew 3650 statute miles in 3 hours 35 minutes and burned 46 pounds per mile, what was the fuel burn rate?
Assuming a jet fuel weight of 6.8 pounds per gallon and an aircraft capacity of 98 passengers, what is the fuel economy in the previous question?
questions.context: Referenced question 29: Fast-forward to 1977 when the crew of an Air France Concorde flew the inaugural trip between New York and Paris. Those smart alecks were really putting old Lindy to shame by cruising at Mach 2.02. If they flew 3650 statute miles in 3 hours 35 minutes and burned 46 pounds per mile, what was the fuel burn rate?

questions.question: Referring back to the Lindberg questions, and realizing that the Spirit of St. Louis only has one seat, what was the fuel economy in miles per gallon of Lindberg's flight?
questions.context: Referenced question 23: When Charles Lindberg crossed the Atlantic Ocean in 1927, he flew for 33.5 hours and traveled 3610 statute miles. What was his average speed? [[DIVIDER]] Referenced question 25: He carried 451 gallons of fuel, and landed with 85 gallons. What was his average fuel burn?

questions.question: The approved CG range for your aircraft is from 22.5% Mean Aerodynamic Chord (MAC) to 34.0% MAC. If the leading edge of MAC is located at station 100" (equivalent to 0% MAC) and the trailing edge is located at station 140" (100% MAC), what station is the leading edge of the approved MAC envelope?

Referencing the previous question, you have completed all your weight and balance paperwork and determine that your CG is at 22.0% MAC. How much weight do you have to move from the front baggage area at station 85" to the aft baggage area at station 210" to move the CG to the forward limit? Your aircraft weighs 8,400 pounds.
questions.context: Referenced question 32: The approved CG range for your aircraft is from 22.5% Mean Aerodynamic Chord (MAC) to 34.0% MAC. If the leading edge of MAC is located at station 100" (equivalent to 0% MAC) and the trailing edge is located at station 140" (100% MAC), what station is the leading edge of the approved MAC envelope?

questions.question: You are cruising at a TAS of 83 knots. You fly a heading of 301° for 8 minutes and notice a drift of 4° right. You then fly a heading of 045° for 6 minutes and drift 5° left. What are the winds aloft?

After flying for 1 hour 22 minutes, you realize you have drifted 4 miles off course. If your ground speed was 133 knots, how many degrees must you turn to arrive directly over your destination which is only 25 minutes away?
You are cruising at a blistering 73 knots ground speed in your C150. How long will it take to fly 2 nautical miles?
It is a rather cool day in Phoenix, only 36°C. If the altimeter is 29.86" and the field elevation is 1,135 MSL, what is your density altitude?
Assuming a standard atmosphere, what is the speed of sound at 35,000 feet?
You have a TAS of 122 knots. The wind is 140° true at 28 knots and your TC is 311°. What is your compass heading if the variation is 14° W and the compass deviation is 2° east?
If you are climbing at 550 feet per minute, what is your climb gradient if your ground speed is 84 knots?
